signal: The Chair Barbers is a two-chair traditional barber in Otley taking walk-ins and phone bookings. The site lists prices and opening hours; there is no online booking or card checkout — customers appear to pay in person by cash or card machine.

Mulberry Court provides adult day care in Wakefield — therapeutic activities, meals and transport for older people. Fees are invoiced to families; there is no online payment option shown, so payments appear to be by cheque or bank transfer.
Ridgeway is a sole physiotherapist offering home visits across Harrogate. Appointments are arranged by phone or email and invoiced afterwards; the site mentions BACS and cash but no card payment.

The Copper Kettle is an independent tearoom in Grassington serving lunches and afternoon teas. It takes table bookings by phone; there is a card machine in the tearoom but no online ordering or payment page.

Fairfield Joinery is a small bespoke joinery workshop near Skipton doing fitted furniture and staircases. Quotes are given by email and jobs invoiced on completion, paid by bank transfer; no online payment is offered.

Pennine Dog Grooming is a one-person grooming salon in Ilkley booked via a Facebook page and phone. Prices are listed; payment is taken in the salon by cash or card reader, with no way to pay or deposit online.
